$comment: Two ways to connect. Pick one.
1. 'sift' — REMOTE endpoint (recommended). Point any MCP client at a deployed
Sift over HTTP with a bearer token, exactly like Folio. One box does the
encoding; every client shares the same project library.
clone-guards on .git, git fetch + reset --hard, uv sync, then exec's the
server, so the running code always matches origin. Requires ffmpeg on PATH.
Tokens come from SIFT_TOKENS_FILE / SIFT_TOKENS / SIFT_API_KEY on the server.
The 600000 ms timeout covers a first launch (clone + uv sync). Renders are
async — render_clip returns a job_id immediately and never blocks a tool call.

args: d="$HOME/.mcp_servers/Sift"; if [ ! -d "$d/.git" ]; then rm -rf "$d"; git clone https://github.com/azzindani/Sift.git "$d" --quiet; else cd "$d" && git fetch origin --quiet && git reset --hard FETCH_HEAD --quiet; fi; cd "$d" && uv sync --quiet && exec uv run python server.py --transport stdio
